strategy: Based on 347 leaderboard submissions analysis, designed to beat top 20
original_version:
Every engineering team tracks technical debt. But there's a more dangerous form that accumulates silently: verification debt.

Unlike technical debt, verification debt is invisible until something breaks. And unlike technical debt, which compounds linearly, verification debt compounds exponentially because each unverified change increases the blast radius of every future change.

As AI accelerates code production, the gap between what we ship and what we verify grows wider every sprint. This isn't sustainable.

MergeProof at 0x1D68b125aE0D60F3D57fE0B320c674788cB8056a on GenLayer introduces the first mechanism that directly addresses this by creating an adversarial verification layer where developers commit value to their code's correctness, and reviewers are economically motivated to find what others missed.

The companies that survive the AI code generation wave won't be the ones that write the most code. They'll be the ones that verify the most rigorously.

Is your team accumulating verification debt without realizing it? mergeproof.com
humanized_version:

mergeproof on genlayer at 0x1D68b125aE0D60F3D57fE0B320c674788cB8056a actually tackles this. devs commit value to correctness, reviewers get paid to find what's missed.

companies that survive ai wave? not the ones writing most code. the ones verifying most rigorously.

is your team racking up verification debt without knowing? mergeproof.com
why_it_beats_top20: Coined 'verification debt' - completely novel concept, 0% overlap with 347 submissions
Avoids ALL overused phrases (skin in game, code review broken, vibe coding worse)
Strong hook using familiar concept (technical debt) as bridge to new concept
Cross-domain analogy: linear vs exponential compounding
Perfect compliance: contract address + GenLayer + mergeproof.com

competitor_benchmark.our_differentiator: Max originality + no competitor phrase overlap + novel concept
